items.title: Provide source material
items.avoid: Asking Claude to summarize a document you never attach or paste.
items.prefer: Upload/paste the text (or use retrieval/tools) and specify audience and length.
items.example: Bad: "Summarize our Q3 report." Good: "Summarize the attached Q3 PDF in 5 bullets for executives."

items.title: Ask for structure on hard tasks
items.avoid: One vague sentence for multi-step analysis.
items.prefer: Request steps, output format, and success criteria.
items.why: Structured prompts improve reasoning and coding outputs.
items.example: "List assumptions, then recommend option A or B with risks" beats "What should we do?"

items.title: Verify high-stakes outputs
items.avoid: Publishing Claude's legal, medical, or financial text without expert review.
items.prefer: Use Claude for drafts and checklists; humans sign off on decisions.
items.example: Use Claude to draft FAQ copy; compliance counsel reviews before publish.
